title: Skip git commit in _git_commit_bead when nothing is staged
description:
In storage.py:_git_commit_bead(), git add is always followed by git commit unconditionally. When the bead JSON has not changed since the last commit (e.g. the feature root/epic bead being re-saved with identical content), git add stages nothing and git commit exits with code 1. This fires a logger.warning and appends a spurious git_commit_failed event to the bead's execution history on every occurrence, cluttering both the scheduler output and the bead audit trail.

Fix: after git add, run 'git diff --cached --quiet' and return early if the exit code is 0 (nothing staged), skipping the git commit entirely. Only attempt git commit when there are actually staged changes.

The change is in _git_commit_bead() in src/agent_takt/storage.py, between the git add and git commit subprocess calls.
acceptance_criteria: When a bead file is saved with identical content to the last commit, no warning is logged and no git_commit_failed event is appended to execution history
When a bead file has genuinely changed, git commit still runs as before
uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q passes
